Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Defendants Deposition Subpoenas For Medical Records; Request For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of David Pivtorak
Off Calendar May-04-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance by any party or counsel.

The tentative ruling is adopted, Plaintiff David Wellausen's Motion To Quash Defendants Deposition Subpoenas For Medical Records; Request For Sanctions is off calendar. The motion was advanced and heard on April 23, 2018.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul A. Renne, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter Not Reported. =(302/JPT/RBU)

Plaintiff, David Wellhausen'S Motion To Quash. (Per Ost Filed 04-16-2018)
Off Calendar Apr-23-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, David Pivtorak of the law offices of Pivtorak Law Firm (213.291.9130) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Ryan J. Greenspan of the law offices of McNamara Ney Beatty Slattery Borges & Ambacher (925-939-5330) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Plaintiff, David Wellhausen's Motion To Quash the subpoena served on Laura Hutto is GRANTED in substantial part and Mr. Wellhausen is awarded monetary sanctions of $2890. The subpoena to Ms. Hutto is limited to all documents related to Mr. Wellhausen's left shoulder for the time period of April 9, 2005 to the present. The subpoena is overbroad. By filing this case, Mr. Wellhausen effected a waiver of his medical privacy only as to the medical condition he seeks to hold defendants liable. Mr. Wellhausen's privacy interest in his medical information outweighs defendants' interest in seeking the information based on speculation that relevant information might be discovered. The positon of defendants as to the scope of the subpoena lacks substantial justification. The court determines that the high end of reasonable fees and costs for this simple motion and the preceding meet and confer is $2890, calculated as eight hours of attorney time at $350 per hour and costs of $90. Monetary sanctions above that amount are excessive and would be punitive, which is contrary to the cost-shifting purpose of discovery monetary sanctions. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, e-mail: torreanom@aol.org (L&M302)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
